claim: Claude Code now supports 1 million token context windows, but hallucinations spike noticeably above 250-300k tokens; memory compaction or session handoff is recommended at that threshold
actionable: Monitor context size with /context command; plan for memory compaction or session breaks around 250k tokens to maintain code quality and avoid hallucinations
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: Agent teams in Claude Code allow parallel agents to communicate and coordinate; they can work on different tasks simultaneously while sharing context (e.g., security + performance + test coverage reviews)
actionable: Use agent teams for proof-of-concept and exploratory multi-angle analysis; avoid production use yet due to suboptimal inter-agent communication
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: Git worktree support in Claude Code creates isolated copies of codebases per feature, enabling true parallel development without branches overriding each other
actionable: Use /create-worktree for parallel feature development; maintain separate worktrees in .claude/worktrees to avoid merge conflicts and enable true parallel work
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: The /simplify command guides the coding agent to reduce over-engineering; use it after any implementation to improve code quality and reduce unnecessary complexity
actionable: After Claude Code generates code, run /simplify to automatically refactor for clarity and reduce over-engineered patterns
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: The /batch command orchestrates large-scale code changes in parallel (e.g., replacing all console.log with a structured logger); splits work and coordinates sub-agents automatically
actionable: For large refactors, use /batch to parallelize the work; describe the refactoring goal and let the system coordinate sub-agents to apply changes across the codebase
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: Remote control feature allows Claude Code conversations to sync with the Claude app; send messages on phone and see them appear on desktop in real-time, enabling work coordination across devices
actionable: Use /remote-control to sync Claude Code sessions with mobile; enables hands-free oversight of long-running tasks from anywhere
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: The /btw command creates a sidecar conversation without bloating the main context window; good for quick questions but Claude has no tools in this mode
actionable: Use /btw for quick knowledge questions to avoid inflating your main context; for code-based exploration, use sub-agents instead
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: The /loop command schedules recurring Claude Code prompts (e.g., every 5 minutes check deployment status, every 30 minutes run tests); enables continuous quality gates and monitoring
actionable: Use /loop to automate recurring checks (tests, deployments, monitoring) and alert hooks if conditions fail; reduces manual polling
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane

claim: Auto memory (distinct from claude.md) lets Claude accumulate knowledge across sessions and learn from mistakes; it's enabled by default in a memory folder within .claude
actionable: Enable auto memory for autonomous learning across sessions; use claude.md for explicit rules if you want deterministic control
source_title: You're Hardly Using What Claude Code Has to Offer, it's Insane
